headline: Agent orchestration and model routing dominated the month as harnesses and judge layers moved to coordinate multiple coding models.
body: Routing and ensemble methods anchored the window: Omnigent positioned itself as a meta-harness that hands tasks between Claude Code, Codex, and Pi, while OpenRouter's Fusion API routed prompts across models and used a judge to synthesize answers, with tested results coming back inconsistent and sometimes slower than a single strong model. The Claude Fable removal pushed attention toward local inference via Ollama and multi-provider routing across Claude, DeepSeek, and others, set against discussion of OpenAI price cuts and whether frontier models are commoditizing. A new cluster of web sandboxing techniques broke out around double iframe isolation, covering sandbox attribute, Content Security Policy, srcdoc, and storage and origin isolation for MCP and ChatGPT apps. Separately, reports framed web traffic as now bot-dominated, with web scraping and autonomous agents browsing and purchasing in place of humans.
